title: Multi-Agent Orchestration
subtitle: Multi-Agent Orchestration
description: Coordinates how multiple specialized agents execute tasks sequentially or in parallel, distributes work, and aggregates results. The core challenge is balancing flexibility with controllability across distributed agent execution.
sub_problems: Task decomposition: Breaking complex tasks into parallelizable subtasks
Execution orchestration: DAG scheduling, sequential/parallel/conditional branching
State synchronization: Sharing context and intermediate results across agents
Result aggregation: Merging results from multiple sub-agents and conflict resolution
Dynamic routing: Selecting appropriate agents based on task type
Pattern selection: Choosing different orchestration modes for different task types within the same system
Iteration convergence: Determining when agent loops should terminate (should_stop signal design)
Dynamic queue expansion: Adding new topics to the scheduling queue when discovered during research
best_practices: Start simple: Single agent with multiple tools is often easier to debug than multiple agents
Define clear role boundaries: Each agent's responsibilities must be distinct to avoid overlap
Make state serializable: State passed between agents must be serializable for persistence and recovery
Implement timeouts and circuit breakers: Parallel agents need timeout mechanisms to prevent single-agent blocking
Validate aggregated results: Check consistency when merging results and handle conflicts
Use federated orchestration: Different modules can use different orchestration patterns without forced uniformity
Lazy initialization: Create non-essential agents only on first use to reduce resource waste
Async wrapper: Provide async wrappers for non-thread-safe components to support smooth parallel mode transitions

values.DeepTutor: Research module Semaphore-controlled parallelism, asyncio.gather batch execution

values.MiroThinker: Independent message_history without polluting main agent
values.DeepTutor: Three-layer Memory persistence + GuidedSession session state machine
asyncio.Semaphore(max_parallel) + asyncio.Lock for shared state protection

values.MiroThinker: YAML-configured independent tool set per sub_agent
values.DeepTutor: Research unified _call_tool routing, Solve isolated via ToolAgent
5 independent orchestrators each selecting optimal orchestration pattern
Solve Loop Agents created on first use only, reducing startup overhead

solutions.title: Lead Agent → SubagentExecutor dynamic delegation with task_tool.py parallel orchestration
solutions.description: Lead Agent dynamically delegates subtasks to Subagents via task_tool.py's task() function. SubagentExecutor (executor.py) uses dual thread pool architecture: _scheduler_pool(3) for scheduling + _execution_pool(3) for execution. SubagentConfig (config.py) defines name/tools/model/timeout with model="inherit" support for parent model reuse. SubagentLimitMiddleware enforces MAX_CONCURRENT_SUBAGENTS=3 limit. Tool isolation via disallowed_tools=["task"] prevents recursive nesting.

solutions.title: DeepResearchSkill tree-based recursion with breadth=4 depth=2 and asyncio.Semaphore(2) concurrency control
solutions.description: gpt_researcher/skills/deep_research.py's DeepResearchSkill implements tree-based recursive research: each layer generates breadth sub-queries, each creating independent GPTResearcher instances executing conduct_research(). Breadth halves per layer max(2, breadth//2) to control exponential growth. asyncio.Semaphore(2) limits concurrency. Cross-layer sharing via visited_urls deduplication + learnings knowledge accumulation. Default parameters breadth=4 depth=2 produce ~36 searches + 20 LLM calls in 3-5 minutes.

solutions.title: run_sub_agent() sub-agents as tools with expose_sub_agents_as_tools() configuration-driven approach
solutions.description: orchestrator.py:327-499's run_sub_agent() executes sub-agents independently with isolated system_prompt, tool_definitions, message_history, and max_turns limits. settings.py's expose_sub_agents_as_tools() exposes sub-agents as callable tools for the main agent. YAML configuration (conf/agent/multi_agent.yaml) defines main_agent and sub_agents (agent-browsing/agent-research/agent-citation), each with independent tool sets and turn limits.

solutions.title: Multi-module dual-loop orchestration pattern
solutions.description: Multiple orchestration modes coexist within the same system, with optimal strategy selected based on task characteristics
